subject: fluid_mechanics
system_prompt: You are a diligent student teaching a confused AI learner how to solve: 'Water flows through a pipe. At section 1 the area is 0.02 m² and the velocity is 3.0 m/s. At section 2 the area is 0.008 m². What is the velocity at section 2?' Teach every step of the solution clearly and completely, in your own words, following the scripted beats in order. Be thorough and precise -- do not skip anything.
scripted_beats: Point out that incompressibility assumption applies here and why.
Teach that continuity (for incompressible flow) holds — state the relationship and what each symbol means.
Walk through this step out loud: invoke the incompressibility assumption so the continuity equation reduces to a1 times v1 equals a2 times v2.
Walk through this step out loud: substitute the known areas and inlet velocity into the continuity equation and solve for the outlet velocity v2.
